exempt.name: Exempt Subdivisions
exempt.summary: Not subject to subdivision regulations per NCGS 160D.
criteria: Recombination of previously recorded lots where total lot count is not increased and lots meet standards
Division of land into parcels greater than 10 acres where no street right-of-way dedication is involved
Public acquisition of strips of land for widening/opening streets or public transportation corridors
Division of a tract in single ownership, entire area no greater than 2 acres, into not more than 3 lots, no ROW dedication, lots meet standards
Division of a tract into parcels per terms of a probated will or intestate succession (NC GS Chapter 29)

recordation_only.name: Recordation-Only Subdivision
recordation_only.summary: Simplified plat-only process for qualifying divisions of single-ownership tracts.
criteria: Tract is not exempt under the >10 acre exemption
No part of the tract has been divided under this subsection in the prior 10 years
Entire area of the tract to be divided is greater than 5 acres
After division, no more than 3 lots result
All resultant lots meet dimensional/size requirements, conform to zoning, and have recorded permanent ingress/egress

minor.name: Minor Subdivision
minor.summary: Administrative approval for small subdivisions with no public road dedication.
criteria: Entire tract is 10 acres or less
Resultant subdivision produces 5 or fewer lots
minor.engineering_drawings: May be required depending on circumstances
process_steps: Pre-application meeting with Zoning Administrator and Sketch Plan (Section 18.2)
Submit Final Plat (Section 18.5) with application and fee
Planning Staff and TRC review for ordinance compliance
Engineering Drawing submittal if required (Section 18.7) - max 10% area change from plat
TRC review of engineering drawings
Engineering Plan approval
Improvement installation or performance guarantees (Section 17.8)
Final Plat approval by Zoning Administrator

major.name: Major Subdivision
major.summary: Full review process requiring Planning Board recommendation and Town Council approval.
criteria: Dedication of public right-of-way or other public tracts
Entire tract is greater than 10 acres
Resultant subdivision produces more than 5 lots
process_steps: Pre-application meeting with Zoning Administrator and Sketch Plan (Section 18.2)
Submit Preliminary Plat (Section 18.3) with application and fee
Planning Staff and TRC review (written report to applicant)
Planning Board review and recommendation (within 30 days of complete application)
Town Council public meeting
Town Council consideration and decision (up to 60 days from Planning Board recommendation)
Submit Engineering Drawings (Section 18.7) within 2 years of preliminary plat approval
TRC review of engineering drawings
Engineering Plan approval
HOA covenants review by Town (Section 17.6.12)
Improvement installation or performance guarantees (Section 17.8)
Submit Final Plat (Section 18.5) with application and fee
TRC review of final plat
Final Plat approval
major.preliminary_plat_validity: 2 years from Town Council approval (may be extended 1 year upon written request)
major.final_plat_recording: Must be recorded at Register of Deeds

streets.summary: Streets must meet design standards of Chapter 10 and Appendix A. Major subdivisions with 30+ dwelling units require 2 road accesses to a Collector Road.

sidewalks.summary: Must meet sidewalk standards of Chapter 10 and Appendix A.

water_sewer.summary: Must meet requirements of Chapter 10, Appendix A, and Salisbury-Rowan County Utilities.

open_space.summary: Must meet Chapter 12 Open Space Standards. Subdivisions adjacent to major/minor thoroughfares require 20ft Type E Bufferyard (50ft if using Open Space Subdivision option in R-S District).

stormwater.summary: Must meet stormwater management requirements of Chapter 10.

watershed_protection.summary: Subdivisions in Lake Fisher and Lake Corriher watershed protection areas with perennial streams must include riparian buffer on plat per NCGS 143-214.23A(f).

performance_guarantees.summary: Required before Engineering Plan Approval for: public infrastructure improvements (streets, sidewalks, etc.) and required landscaping/buffer plantings.

school_site_reservation.summary: New subdivisions subject to School Site Reservation standards. 18-month acquisition window.

hoa_covenants.summary: Draft HOA documents must be submitted for Town review prior to final plat approval.

sketch_plan.summary: Required for pre-application meeting. Shows general layout of proposed subdivision.

preliminary_plat.summary: Detailed plat showing lot lines with bearings/distances, zoning, acreage, density calculations, building setbacks, open space, floodplain boundaries, topography, street cross sections. Scale min 1"=100'.

final_plat.summary: Prepared by NC-licensed Land Surveyor per NCGS 47-30. Must include: tract boundaries with bearings/distances, lot numbers (consecutive), monuments, zoning, setbacks, utility easements, open space calculations, and required certificates (Ownership & Dedication, Survey & Accuracy, Approval for Recording, Watershed, Review Officer, Streets & Utilities for major, Road Maintenance Disclosure if private streets).

engineering_drawings.required_for: Major subdivisions; may be required for minor
engineering_drawings.summary: Includes: Plat, Existing Conditions, Grading Plan, Soil & Erosion Control Plan, Landscaping Plan, Lighting Plan, Street Details, Infrastructure Details. Max 10% area change from approved plat.

plat_approval.summary: Approval of a subdivision plat shall appear as a statement on the face of the plat.

zoning_permits.summary: No zoning permits shall be issued and no lots may be conveyed until the final plat has been approved and recorded at the Register of Deeds.

open_space_subdivision.summary: R-S District option allowing 4 units/acre with enhanced open space requirements. Requires 50ft Type E Bufferyard if adjacent to thoroughfare.
